tasks.title: Implement hello function with test suite and package config
tasks.description: Create the complete Node.js hello-world smoke test with all three required files: hello.js (pure function), hello.test.js (native node:test), and package.json (minimal config). This validates the v2.2 generic harness shell-out runner with the simplest possible implementation.
acceptanceCriteria: hello.js file exists at repository root
hello.js exports a named function called 'hello'
hello function is pure (no console output, no side effects)
hello.test.js file exists at repository root
hello.test.js uses node:test module (no external test frameworks)
package.json file exists at repository root
package.json has no dependencies or devDependencies entries
tasks.testingApproach: Run 'npm test' from repository root. Verify exit code 0. Verify only three files created. Verify hello function returns correct greeting format with different inputs.

CODE: README.md (/Users/jackjin/dev/harness-v2-test/README.md)
# harness-v2-test

Monorepo for projects generated by autonomous harness pipelines.

Each project lives in its own directory under `projects/`. Projects are generated by various harness systems (study harness, app builders, etc.) and run independently.
Deployments are published to GitHub Pages under `https://jackzhaojin.github.io/harness-v2-test/projects/<project>/`.

## Structure

```
projects/
├── aem-devops-cert-v1/  ← AEM DevOps certification study environment
├── claude-cert-v1/      ← Claude certification study environment
└── (future projects...)
```

## Projects

| Project | Generator | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| `aem-devops-cert-v1` | study-harness-v2026-03-v1 | Interactive study environment for the Adobe AEM DevOps Engineer Expert exam |
